criteria.text: Two primary views are implemented: Garage and Winners
The Garage view displays its name, current page number, and total number of cars in the database
The Winners view displays its name, current page number, and total number of records in the winners table
View state is preserved when navigating between views: page number, input values, and selected color are not reset
CRUD for cars works: create, update, delete, list. Deleting a car removes it from both garage and winners tables
Color is selected from an RGB palette and the selected color is reflected on the car's image
Update and delete buttons are placed next to each car
Pagination is implemented with 7 cars per page
A button creates 100 random cars per click. Names are assembled from two random parts with at least 10 options per part; color is also random
Start/stop engine buttons are placed next to each car
Start engine animation - UI awaits the velocity response, animates the car, then issues the 'drive' request. On 500 from 'drive', the animation stops in place
Stop engine animation - UI awaits the stop response and the car returns to its initial position
Button states - start is disabled while driving; stop is disabled at the initial position
Animations remain fluid and visible at a 500px viewport width
Start race button starts the race for all cars on the current page
Reset race button returns all cars on the current page to their starting positions
After the first car finishes, a winner message containing the car's name is shown
After a race, the winning car appears in the Winners table
Pagination is implemented with 10 winners per page
The table has columns: №, image, name, number of wins, best time (in seconds). Win count increments on repeat wins; best time is updated only if the new time is better
Sorting by number of wins and by best time works in both ascending and descending order
A forbidden UI library or framework is used (React, Vue, Angular, jQuery, Lodash, Material Design, etc.)
TypeScript is not used, or 'any' / type assertions ('as') / non-null assertions ('!') are used
Functions exceed 40 lines or the project does not pass the unicorn ESLint configuration
Major bug - implemented functionality works but after some manipulations it breaks down, with unexpected errors in the browser console (per bug)

Minor bug - implemented functionality works but after some manipulations behavior changes (e.g. a button does not become enabled after a state change) and there are no errors in the browser console (per bug)
